topic: Two colleagues are both considered for the same promotion
raw_output:
[story 1]

The fluorescent lights of the office hummed a monotonous tune, a soundtrack to my rising anxiety. I stared at the framed memo detailing the promotion criteria, the glossy paper feeling impossibly heavy in my hands. Across the cubicle farm, Sarah was laughing, a bright, unrestrained sound that seemed to mock my stillness. Every time my name was mentioned in hushed tones by management, a cold knot tightened in my stomach, constricting my chest until breathing felt shallow. I found myself meticulously re-reading old performance reviews, searching for some flaw, some minuscule error that would justify the other person’s selection. My jaw ached from clenching it so tightly.

[story 2]

"Another update on the leadership track?" I managed, my voice sounding unnaturally high-pitched even to my own ears. The air in the breakroom felt thick, suffocating. When Mark spoke, his tone was warm, almost sympathetic, which only amplified the sharp, acidic feeling churning inside me. I fiddled incessantly with the edge of my coffee mug, tracing the chipped rim with my thumb until the ceramic felt slick. I kept glancing toward the hallway, half-expecting to see Sarah walking by, a silent judgment in her posture, a silent acknowledgment of the invisible contest raging between us.

[story 3]

My hands were slick with sweat as I tried to focus on the quarterly projections. The numbers swam before my eyes, irrelevant compared to the singular, agonizing focus on the decision looming ahead. I stood up abruptly, needing movement, and paced the length of my desk, the worn carpet muffling my hurried footsteps. Each step was a forced effort, a desperate attempt to burn off the internal heat. When I finally sat back down, I pressed my palms flat against the cool surface of my desk, a silent plea for the pressure to simply dissipate, to leave me alone with the quiet, dreadful knowledge of what might be lost.
